present_usage.summary: Expressing much in few words; brief but meaningful.

examples.text: The word concise changes what claims the agent may safely make.

cultural_frame.summary: concise appears in communication where tone, risk, evidence, or commitment must be controlled.

frames.description: Frames concise as a term whose pragmatic force can change the safe output path.

pragmatics.summary: Concise language helps scanning but can omit required facts if over-compressed.

expansion.paragraph: concise is represented here as a semantic-control term: its present usage and pragmatic force help decide whether an agent should ask, check evidence, avoid overclaiming, or revise before export.
